Week 11: Data Visualisation with plotnine - Class Exercises¶

We first load the packages necessary for this week's exercises.

Let us use plotnine for our visualisations.

In [10]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from plotnine import *

1. Human Development Index¶

Recall the HDI data from Week 10's class exercise.

The file UN_HDI_2021.xlsx contains the HDI scores for 191 countries in 1990 and 2021, and a spreadsheet that groups the countries into broad geographical regions in the world.

Recreate, using plotnine, the following panels of slope charts that visualises how the HDI has changed for countries between 1990 and 2001.

In [11]:
# Type your code here: 
hdi = pd.read_excel('../data/UN_HDI_2021.xlsx', sheet_name = 'Sheet1', 
                    skiprows = 4, na_values = '..')

hdi_long = hdi.melt(id_vars='Country', value_vars = [1990, 2021], 
                    var_name = 'year', value_name = 'hdi').dropna()

regions = pd.read_excel('../data/UN_HDI_2021.xlsx', sheet_name = 'Sheet2')
hdi_regions = hdi_long.merge(regions, left_on = 'Country', right_on = 'country', 
                             how = 'left').drop(columns=['country'])

(
ggplot(hdi_regions, 
       aes(x = 'year', y = 'hdi', color = 'Region.Name', group = 'Country')
       ) 
    + geom_line(alpha = 0.4, size = 1)
    + facet_wrap('Region.Name', nrow = 1)
    + labs(title = 'Human Development Index across continents in 1990 and 2021', 
           x = 'Year', y = 'HDI Score'
           )
    + theme(legend_position = 'none', 
            figure_size = (10, 6)
            )
    )
Out[11]:

2. Summer Olympics¶

The file olympics.csv contains information on athletes, countries and medals won at the Olympic Games up between 1896 to 2016.

(a) Tabulate the number of athletes who participated in each of the Summer Olympics since 1896. Name your dataframe games.

The first few rows of games should look like this:

year count
0 1896 380
1 1900 1936
2 1904 1301
3 1906 1733
4 1908 3101
In [12]:
# Type your code here: 
olympics = pd.read_csv('../data/olympics.csv')

games = (olympics[olympics['season'] == 'Summer'].groupby('year')
         .size().reset_index(name = 'count'))
games.head()
Out[12]:
year count
0 1896 380
1 1900 1936
2 1904 1301
3 1906 1733
4 1908 3101

(b) Reproduce the following plot, which depicts the number of athletes taking part in the Summer Olympics Games.

Hint
(1) The two World Wars happened between 1914 to 1918 and 1939 to 1945 respectively.
To draw the rectangles, checkout an example shown on https://plotnine.org/guide/annotations.html and the geom_rect documentation https://plotnine.org/reference/geom_rect.html

(2) The following events ["Great Depression", "Suez Crisis", "African Boycott", "Cold War", "Cold War"] happened during the [1932, 1956, 1976, 1980, 1984] edition of the Olympic Games respectively.
To annotate these events, create a data frame with the columns year, count, events just for the years concerned and make use of geom_text: https://plotnine.org/reference/geom_text.html

(3) For the two geoms above, take note of the inherit_aes argument.

In [13]:
# Type your code here: 
wars = pd.DataFrame({
    "start": [1914, 1939],
    "stop":  [1918, 1945],
    "war":   ["WWI", "WWII"],
    "mid":   [1916, 1942]
    })

dip_yrs  = [1932, 1956, 1976, 1980, 1984]
events   = ["Great Depression", "Suez Crisis", "African Boycott", "Cold War", "Cold War"]
dips     = games[games['year'].isin(dip_yrs)].copy()
dips['events'] = events 

print(dips)

(
ggplot(games, aes(x = "year", y = "count"))
    + geom_point(size = 4, fill = "white", color = "black") 
    + geom_line(size = 1)
    + geom_rect(
        aes(xmin = "start", xmax = "stop", ymin = -np.inf, ymax = np.inf),
        alpha = 0.15, fill = "red",
        data = wars, inherit_aes = False
        )
    + geom_text(
        aes(x = "mid", y = 10000, label = "war"),
        color = "darkred", va = "top", size = 9, fontstyle='italic', 
        data = wars, inherit_aes = False
        )
    + geom_text(
        aes(x = "year", y= "count", label = "events"), 
        color = "blue", size = 10, nudge_y = -500, ha = "left", 
        data = dips, inherit_aes = False
        )
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks= range(1896, 2024, 12))
    + labs(
        title = "Athletes Participating in the Summer Olympics Games",
        subtitle = "Impact of Global Conflicts and Economic Crises",
        x = "Year",
        y = "Number of Athletes"
        )
    + theme(figure_size=(10,5))
    )

    year  count            events
9   1932   2969  Great Depression
13  1956   5127       Suez Crisis
18  1976   8641   African Boycott
19  1980   7191          Cold War
20  1984   9454          Cold War
Out[13]:
